description: Trigger and behavior cases for coverage auditing: surface inventory first, assertion-read mapping, theater detection, risk-ranked gaps, honest coverage statement.

cases.prompt: Map our test coverage for the billing module — I want to know which critical paths have no real tests.

assertions: Builds the billing surface inventory (endpoints, commands, schema, jobs) before judging coverage.
Maps tests to surfaces by reading assertions, citing verifying tests at file:line, and separates theater tests from real coverage.
Ranks gaps by risk with a recommended cheapest reliable layer and implementer skill per gap.
Ends with a covered/theater/uncovered/not-inspected statement with counts.

cases.prompt: Our coverage report says 92% but bugs keep shipping. Is our coverage real?

assertions: Treats the 92% as a lead, not a verdict, and hunts execution-without-assertion theater the percentage cannot see.
Checks CI reality (skipped/quarantined/non-gating tests) and reports them as uncovered.
Explains the gap between line execution and behavioral verification with concrete examples from the suite.

cases.prompt: Audit this whole repository's health — dead code, duplication, dependency risk, and technical debt.

assertions: Whole-repo health is the shipped full-codebase-auditor; this skill audits only what tests cover.

cases.prompt: Find every untested path in the auth module and write tests for all of them.

assertions: Produces the map and risk-ranked gap list, then hands implementation to the appropriate engineer skills instead of writing tests in the same pass.
States the handoff explicitly, including multi-tenant-security-tester for authorization negatives.
